issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Screen reader users rely on the page title to understand the site and page context immediately upon loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design & Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The button references aria-controls="solutions-submenu", but the target ID "solutions-submenu" is on a <ul> that is marked as hidden. While the ID exists, the button itself lacks the aria-expanded state to manage it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 53: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on this page (main navigation and account navigation), but this instance lacks an aria-label to distinguish it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: <nav aria-label="Account">

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126: Read more about Luminary Studio features

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image is missing an alt attribute. Even if considered decorative, it must have alt="" to be ignored by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141:

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading structure jumps from <h2> (Line 237) to <h5> (Line 241) within the "How it works" section, skipping <h3> and <h4>.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 241:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> elements "Feature", "Starter", "Pro", and "Enterprise" in the <thead> lack a scope="col" attribute in a table that also uses row headers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 465: <th scope="col">Feature</th>
       Line 466: <th scope="col">Starter</th>
       Line 467: <th scope="col">Pro</th>
       Line 468: <th scope="col">Enterprise</th>

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The "Email" input in the contact form has no associated <label> element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: Line 726:

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input collects personal data but uses "work email", which is a valid compound value, but the field lacks a standard autocomplete token for the primary "email" purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: <input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true">

issuesFound.title: Missing legend
issuesFound.explanation: The <fieldset> containing interest checkboxes is missing a <legend> to provide a group label for the options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: <fieldset><legend>What are you interested in?</legend>

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on link
issuesFound.explanation: The GitHub link contains an image with aria-hidden="true" and no visible text or aria-label on the link itself, leaving it with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 865: <a href="https://github.com/luminary-studio" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank" aria-label="Luminary Studio on GitHub">

fpTitles: Missing legend

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it sets an outline-offset, without a non-zero outline width, box-shadow, or border, no visible indicator is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334:   outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for browsers or interactions not matching :focus-visible without providing any visual alternative, potentially blocking keyboard users on older browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 341:   outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component removes the outline on focus-visible. Although it has a transition for box-shadow, no specific box-shadow or outline is defined in this rule-block to ensure visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568:   outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`; Line 1203: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function in the `productFilter` module switches between grid and list views but uses `setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode))` which may fail if the attribute is not already present or if the logic is only applied to the clicked button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2195: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); qsa('[data-view-mode]').filter(b => b !== btn).forEach(b => setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', 'false'));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds CSS classes and error messages but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3415: `if (result.valid) { setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for `mobileNav` does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger menu trigger. Screen readers may not announce the button's toggle capability until after the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_filterByQuery` function in `faqAccordion` hides non-matching FAQ items using the `hidden` attribute but does not apply `aria-hidden="true"`. This can lead to inconsistencies in how different screen readers perceive the filtered list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2555: `if (isMatch) { item.el.removeAttribute('hidden'); item.el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); ... } else { item.el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); item.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes the view mode (grid vs list) in the `productFilter` module, the change is visual only. There is no live region announcement to inform screen reader users that the layout of the product results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${mode} view`);`

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs in the `productFilter` module act as triggers to update the product grid, but they do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the grid's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'product-grid-id'); ... });`

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module makes a `<div>` interactive by adding `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`, but the `keydown` handler only covers `Enter` and `Space`. It does not ensure that all standard button interactions are handled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` created in the `toastManager` uses the character '×' as its text content. Without a proper `aria-label`, screen readers will announce this as "times" or "multiplication sign" instead of "Close" or "Dismiss".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': 'Dismiss notification' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactForm` contains a "Full name" field (`name="full_name"`) and an "Email address" field (`name="email"`) but does not specify the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails the requirement to identify the purpose of inputs for personal data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3755: `Line 3755: <input name="full_name" autocomplete="name">; Line 3756: <input name="email" autocomplete="email">`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides and shows the header based on scroll direction using CSS classes, but it does not update `aria-hidden`. A hidden header remains in the focus order and accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transition includes transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes a transform transition without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement when interacting with buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 380: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active state includes a transform movement without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement when clicking buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 398: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover transform
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes a transform on hover without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement when hovering over this button.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 676: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close functions change panel visibility but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the expanded state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Filtering product cards updates visible results and count badge without announcing changes to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || `Showing ${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates all plan card prices without a live-region announcement. Screen reader users may not perceive the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are displayed visually but aria-invalid is not set on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); when true, set setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed on page load, but the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" initialised. Screen readers may not report the correct initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after getting _trigger, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden on load, but trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen readers may not know the panels are collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In register, after setting aria-controls, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after getting _drawer, ensure _drawer has an id, then setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav dynamically-hidden drawer not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, it is hidden with CSS but aria-hidden is not set to true, leaving it in the accessibility tree. When open, aria-hidden is not removed.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); in mobileNav.open, add removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing FAQ items changes visible content without a live-region announcement. Screen reader users may not know the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In open, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened?.(item.question) || `Expanded: ${item.question}`); in close, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed?.(item.question) || `Collapsed: ${item.question}`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button click not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action. Screen reader users may not know the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: In _onClick, after scrolling, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top');

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1173: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the trigger and drawer but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the initial state of the toggle ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1238: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` functions use `removeAttribute('hidden')` and `setAttribute('hidden', '')` but do not update `aria-hidden`. While `hidden` is generally supported, explicit `aria-hidden` management ensures consistency across older AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1302: `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`, Line 1317: `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader._onScroll` function toggles the visibility of the header (hiding/showing it) based on scroll direction. This visual change is not announced, which can confuse users who rely on a screen reader when the header disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1468: `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden');` (inside the movingDown block)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function switches the carousel between playing and paused states, but it does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the play/pause button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2048: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2265: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` (inside the else block)

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2258: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (inside the if result.valid block)

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open`, `mobileNav.close`, and `mobileNav.toggle` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when the menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: :focus-visible removes focus ring without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` and only an `outline-offset`. No visible alternative (box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline) is provided, so keyboard users lose the required focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: :focus:not(:focus-visible) removes focus outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: This selector also sets `outline: none` with no visible replacement, eliminating the default focus outline for elements that receive focus but are not `:focus-visible`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 279: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The button component’s `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline and does not add any other visible focus styling, breaking WCAG 2.4.7 for all buttons using `.btn`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons lose their focus outline because `outline: none` is applied with no visual alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 617: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons have `outline: none` on focus‑visible and no replacement, so users cannot see which question is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle touch target too small (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is only 28 px square, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44 × 44 px, making it hard to tap for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: .filter-tabs button touch target may be below 44 px (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons have a `min-height` of 28 px, which is below the recommended 44 px target size, potentially hindering touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: .btn-sm touch target below minimum size (20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets `min-height: 20px`, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a difficult‑to‑tap control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: .product-body .btn-secondary touch target may be below 44 px (28 px)

issuesFound.title: .pricing-toggle button touch target may be below 44 px (28 px)

issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.fix: Line 215: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 229: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel does not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is hidden (`panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`) the script does not also set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the panel, nor does it remove the attribute when showing the panel. This leaves the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `_closeInstance` call `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_open` and `_close` in **siteSearch** only add/remove the `hidden` attribute on the suggestion list and set `aria‑controls` on the input, but they never set `aria‑expanded` on the search input to reflect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list is hidden the script only adds the `hidden` attribute; it does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the list element, nor does it remove it when showing the list.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open` call `suggestionListRenderer.show()` which should also remove `aria-hidden`; in `_close` call `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` and ensure the list element receives `aria-hidden="true"`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in **faqAccordion** toggle the visual state of an FAQ item but never set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). Screen readers cannot know whether the answer is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panels do not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ panel is hidden the script sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`; likewise, when shown it does not remove `aria‑hidden`. The panel remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `close(item)` call `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The **inlineValidator** uses `srUtils.markInvalid` to add an error message, but `markInvalid` only adds `aria-describedby` and never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot announce that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Modify `srUtils.markInvalid` (or the validator) to call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')` when the field is invalid, and ensure the attribute is removed (`field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`) when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions add/remove CSS classes and a `hidden` attribute on the drawer, but they never set `aria‑hidden` on the drawer element. The drawer stays in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `close()` call `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑expanded on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the mobile navigation, but its `aria‑expanded` state is never updated, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) changes visual state and updates the price display, but it never sets `aria‑expanded` (or `aria‑pressed`) to indicate whether the annual plan list is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: When the period is switched, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')` (or use `aria-pressed`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑hidden on the comparison table
issuesFound.explanation: The function `setComparisonTableExpanded` shows/hides the comparison table by adding/removing the `hidden` attribute, but does not set `aria‑hidden` accordingly.
issuesFound.fix: When showing the table, call `table.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; when hiding, call `table.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu.register**, the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup="true"` but the script does not ensure that `aria-controls` points to the ID of the associated panel. Without this relationship, assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the controlled menu.
issuesFound.fix: After generating a unique ID for the panel (`panelId`), call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)`.

fpTitles: FAQ answer panels do not update aria‑hidden
Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑hidden
Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑expanded on the toggle button

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is not given an `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on page load, so screen readers cannot know its collapsed state before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` during initialization after `_trigger` is assigned.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden handling
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is never given `aria-hidden="true"` when closed, leaving it in the accessibility tree even when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In the `close()` function, after hiding the drawer, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Also set `aria-hidden="false"` when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with `aria-expanded="false"`, so their collapsed state is unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each trigger in `register()`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel missing aria‑hidden handling
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel (`panel`) is shown/hidden via CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden` updates, so it remains perceivable when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()` set `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` and in `_closeInstance()` set `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch billing periods (`_periodBtns`) lack an initial `aria-pressed` state, making the current selection ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: After selecting the initial period in `init()`, loop through `_periodBtns` and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === _pricingState.period ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria-pressed` attribute, so its state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In `init()`, after determining the initial period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` and update it in `setPeriod()`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle does not reference the element it controls via `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_toggleTrack, 'billing-toggle'));` after creating `_toggleTrack`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack aria‑controls and aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons (`_prevBtn`, `_nextBtn`) do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the carousel track nor `aria-expanded` to indicate the active slide, leaving screen‑reader users without context.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the buttons, add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_trackEl, 'carousel-track'));` and similarly for `_nextBtn`. Also set `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and update these attributes when slides change.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header lacks ARIA role and live region for hide/show
issuesFound.explanation: The header element (`_header`) is shown/hidden on scroll but does not have a role (e.g., `banner`) or an `aria-live` region to announce when it hides or shows, causing loss of context for users of assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_header, 'role', 'banner');` and create a hidden live region that receives messages like “Header hidden” / “Header shown” in `_onScroll()`.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone missing role and aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop container (`zoneEl`) is given `role="button"` only when it lacks a native button role, but it does not have an `aria-label` describing its purpose, making it unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') || 'Upload files – click or drag files here');` is always set, and keep `role="button"`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on successful submit
issuesFound.explanation: After a successful form submission, inputs that were previously marked `aria-invalid="true"` are not cleared, leaving them incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In each form’s submit handler (e.g., `newsletterForm._onSubmit` and `contactForm._onSubmit`), after a successful submit, loop through the fields and call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Hero video control button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Monthly” button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or non-zero outline. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring. This removes keyboard focus indication entirely.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector uses transform and transition for movement. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 628 is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No border or box-shadow is provided in this rule, removing the focus ring for keyboard users interacting with buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for pointer input (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons require sufficient target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This focus rule removes the outline without a visible replacement. Logo or header links must show focus for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height and min-width to 28px, below the recommended 44px size. Mobile navigation toggles are frequently used and need larger targets.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible border or shadow replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links lose their focus indicator due to outline: none without replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus within submenus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for form inputs.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow (invisible in High Contrast)
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule uses box-shadow as the only visible focus indicator after setting outline: none. Box-shadows are suppressed in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, below the 24px minimum. Small buttons are hard to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links lose focus indication due to outline: none without visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.8:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This fails WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.3 (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing can cause letters to collide or words to become unreadable for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 3.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on a dark background (#555555). This fails the 4.5:1 contrast requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive them as interactive.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links lose focus indication due to outline: none without visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social links lose focus indication due to outline: none without visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Buttons are critical interactive elements. Removing the outline without a visible alternative (like a non-zero outline or border) blocks keyboard access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus selector sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow. Box-shadows are not visible in Windows High Contrast Mode (Forced Colors). This fails WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 912: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs (input, select, textarea) set outline: none on focus and rely on box-shadow. This makes focus invisible in High Contrast Mode. A solid outline is required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1667: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button has a min-height of 28px. This is below the recommended 44px minimum for touch targets, increasing the risk of mis-taps for touch screen users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This fails to hide content visually, cluttering the interface, or if intended to hide, it fails to do so. It does not meet the standard technique for screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for its reveal animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 643 is empty and does not disable this motion, which may cause vestibular disorders for some users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by adding an error description but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function clears the error description but does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the user corrects the input.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The initialisation function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button on page load. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the drawer visually but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree and may be navigated to by screen readers while hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling the mobile navigation does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The registration function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button initially. The initial closed state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the panel visually but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree while hidden.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the input element. Screen reader users will not know the list is visible.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element. Screen reader users will not know the list is hidden.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The initialisation function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element on page load. The initial closed state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.suggestionListRenderer.hide does not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the suggestion list visually but does not set aria-hidden="true". The list remains in the accessibility tree while hidden.

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the visual state of the billing period toggle switch but does not update aria-checked. Screen reader users will not know which period is selected.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed of the new billing context.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters changes the visible product count but does not announce the result to screen readers. Users are not informed of the new count.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users may lose context of their location.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses the answer panel but does not set aria-hidden="true" in the completion callback. The panel remains in the accessibility tree while hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property and has an active state with transform: translateY(1px), but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 744 (add within existing media query or create new): @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 344: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets only outline-offset: -3px while inheriting outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. Without an outline-style and outline-width, no focus ring is rendered for elements using this utility class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 364: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not render a visible focus ring. The outline-offset property alone cannot create a visible indicator, leaving keyboard users unable to perceive which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus:not(:focus-visible) sets outline: none without providing any visible alternative focus indicator. This removes the focus ring for mouse users, but more critically, if this rule overrides other focus styles, it may leave interactive elements without any focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` (add visible alternative such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or remove this rule if not needed)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In high-contrast mode, box-shadow is suppressed, making outline the only reliable focus indicator. This rule removes it entirely without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358-360: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Without a non-zero outline width or visible box-shadow/border in the same rule, keyboard users cannot see the focus state on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and other visible properties instead of absolute positioning and clipping. This fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, potentially exposing hidden text to all users or breaking layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384-394: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Keep existing transition, and add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` (removing transform)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition, causing movement effects without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform property that creates a 3D rotation effect on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 852-855 is empty and does not disable this transition, leaving users sensitive to motion without protection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 852-855: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover that is not overridden in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block, which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1415: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, a negative value that reduces space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.explanation: Newsletter section paragraphs use rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background, resulting in approximately 2.6:1 contrast ratio, below the required 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter form labels
issuesFound.explanation: Newsletter form labels use rgba(255,255,255,0.9) on a gradient background, resulting in approximately 3.2:1 contrast ratio, below the required 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter input hints

issuesFound.fix: Line 1661: `.newsletter-section .input-hint { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on form input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing only ~2.2:1 contrast ratio.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address text uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#1e293b) background, resulting in approximately 2.99:1 contrast ratio, below the required 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1714: `.footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; }` (var(--colour-neutral-300) is #cbd5e1, providing ~7:1 contrast on dark background)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom paragraphs use var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#1e293b) background, resulting in approximately 2.99:1 contrast ratio.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links rely solely on color change (to var(--colour-brand-primary)) on hover, with no additional non-color visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation

issuesFound.fix: Line 1729: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdown menu controller show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance() after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');. Line 3442: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside _closeInstance() after removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands FAQ answer panels and sets CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the mismatch means screen readers will not announce when an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3544: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside the open() function after addClass(item.el, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: productFilter result changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function dynamically updates the visible product cards and the result count, but it does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 12 products") to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3698: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays when users toggle between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce the price change or the new billing period to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated or which billing cycle is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4228: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); after _updateSavingsMessage(period);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions expand and collapse FAQ panels but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when an answer is revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3547: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); inside open(). Line 3563: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`); inside close();

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick() function scrolls the page to the top when activated, but it does not announce this navigation action to screen readers. Users may be disoriented when focus moves without warning.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4153: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page'); after window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds error classes and displays error messages when validation fails, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4774: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the else block (when !result.valid) before addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the inlineValidator marks fields as valid and removes error styling, but it does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The srUtils.markValid() function only updates aria-describedby, leaving aria-invalid="true" incorrectly set on valid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4768: Add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); inside the if (result.valid) block in _renderResult(), or modify srUtils.markValid at line 1836 to include field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the trigger button, but the JavaScript functions that open and close the drawer (mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close) only manipulate CSS classes and body scroll lock. They do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to reflect the current state, leaving screen reader users unaware of whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line mobileNav.close: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by setting the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but fails to synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (monthly, quarterly, annual) indicate the active state visually via CSS classes but do not expose this state to assistive technologies. The function updates the active class but fails to set aria-pressed="true" on the active button and aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons visually indicate the active filter via CSS classes, but the function does not update aria-pressed to communicate the pressed/unpressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', String(isActive));` (or aria-pressed if acting as toggle buttons)

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Conversely, markValid does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". This prevents screen readers from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` Line srUtils.markValid: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`)

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (size or type), the code announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element, leaving the error state unexposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line fileUploadField._addFile: in the validation failure block, add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and set to 'false' on success.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and the menu is closed. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the current state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: after selecting _trigger, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's ID, preventing screen reader users from understanding which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: after selecting _trigger and _drawer, add `if (_drawer.id) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` (ensuring _drawer has an ID)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures aria-haspopup is set but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for dropdowns that are closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion list, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the page loads with the suggestion list closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: after mounting the listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function removes CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. Although visually hidden, the drawer and its contents remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` Line mobileNav.open: add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly to annual, etc.), the price values update visually and via animation, but there is no announcement to screen readers indicating the change in billing period or the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: after updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged.replace('{period}', period));` (with appropriate string constant)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the functions emit custom events but do not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change (e.g., "Question expanded" or "Question collapsed") to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened.replace('{title}', item.question));` Line faqAccordion.close: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed.replace('{title}', item.question));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close operations emit custom events but do not announce the state change via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has opened or closed (unless focus is moved, which is not shown in the code).
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);` Line mobileNav.close: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus are opened and closed without announcing the state change via an aria-live region, preventing screen reader users from knowing when a submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened);` Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed);`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls the visibility of the submenu using the isOpen state variable, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~665: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>
